questions.question: Who was the founder of the Maurya Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire around 322 BCE, establishing one of the largest empires in Indian history.

questions.question: During the Mughal period, which emperor built the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan commissioned the construction of the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885, marking the beginning of organized political nationalism in India.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Salt Satyagraha in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Salt Satyagraha, a pivotal movement against salt laws in 1930.

questions.question: When did India gain independence from British rule?
questions.explanation: India gained independence from British rule on August 15, 1947.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari is the longest river in India, flowing approximately 1,465 km.

questions.question: Which desert is located in western India?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert lies in western India and southeastern Pakistan.

questions.question: Which Indian state has the highest rainfall?
questions.explanation: Kerala receives the highest rainfall among Indian states due to the southwest monsoon.

questions.question: Which resource is most abundantly found in the Rajasthan state?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is rich in limestone deposits, essential for cement production.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the current President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12 to 35 of the Indian Constitution define and protect Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Who is the current Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Om Birla has been serving as the Speaker of the Lok Sabha since 19 June 2019.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the Parliament?
questions.explanation: Part V of the Indian Constitution deals with the Union and Parliament.

questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental duty of Indian citizens?
questions.explanation: Promoting harmony of the spirit of common brotherhood among all people is a fundamental duty.

questions.question: As of 2026, who is the current Finance Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Nirmala Sitharaman is the current Finance Minister since 30 May 2019.

questions.question: What does GDP stand for?
questions.explanation: GDP stands for Gross Domestic Product, a key indicator of economic activity.

questions.question: India's five-year plans were originally adopted from which country's planning model?
questions.explanation: India adopted its Five Year Plans based on the Soviet Union's planning model.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the largest share to India's GDP?
questions.explanation: The services sector contributes the largest share to India’s GDP.

questions.question: Which element is primarily responsible for the green color in plants?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is responsible for the green color of plants and plays a vital role in photosynthesis.

questions.question: The law of conservation of mass was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Antoine Lavoisier proposed the law of conservation of mass.

questions.question: Which satellite was launched by India in 2019 for navigation purposes?
questions.explanation: NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) is India's satellite navigation system launched for accurate positioning.

questions.question: Which biological process is responsible for the exchange of gases in the human lungs?
questions.explanation: Diffusion allows gases like oxygen and carbon dioxide to exchange in the lungs.

questions.question: Which is the world's largest biodiversity hotspot?
questions.explanation: The Indo-Burma region is considered the world's largest biodiversity hotspot.

questions.question: The Kyoto Protocol is related to which environmental issue?
questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions to combat climate change.

questions.question: Which of the following are protected areas designated for the conservation of biodiversity?
questions.explanation: Both National Parks and Wildlife Sanctuaries are protected areas for conserving biodiversity.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current Vice President of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: C. P. Radhakrishnan has been serving as Vice President since 12 September 2025.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been the Prime Minister since 26 May 2014.

questions.question: Which scheme was launched by the Government of India to promote digital payments?
questions.explanation: Digital India is a flagship initiative to promote digital payments and digital infrastructure.

questions.question: Which global sports event was hosted by India in 2023?
questions.explanation: India hosted the 19th Asian Games in 2023 in Hangzhou, China, with significant participation.
